title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) - Easy Authentic Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make the best authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) at home. This easy, hearty, and healthy recipe brings Middle Eastern flavors to your table. Get cooking!

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A comforting bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh herbs and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this traditional Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas), a flavorful and nutritious Middle Eastern classic perfect for any meal.

description: Experience the heartwarming essence of Lebanese cuisine with this traditional Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas. This incredibly flavorful and nourishing soup is a staple in Middle Eastern households, cherished for its simplicity and wholesome ingredients. A blend of earthy lentils, aromatic spices, and a bright lemon finish makes it a comforting dish perfect for any day.

instructions.title: Prepare the Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic, diced carrots, and diced celery. Cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables begin to soften slightly. Add the cumin powder and turmeric powder, stirring for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Introduce Lentils and Liquid
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine with the vegetables and spices. Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have broken down, and the vegetables are soft. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend (Optional for Smoother Texture)
instructions.text: For a smoother consistency, use an immersion blender to blend a portion of the soup directly in the pot, or carefully transfer about half of the soup to a regular blender, blend until smooth, and return it to the pot. This step is optional; a chunkier soup is also traditional.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding more lemon juice if desired for a brighter flavor.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley and, if using, fresh chopped mint. Serve immediately with a side of pita bread or a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, caramelize the onions a bit longer until golden brown before adding other vegetables. This deepens the savory notes of the soup.
Always taste and adjust the lemon juice at the end. The brightness of fresh lemon is crucial for authentic Lebanese Adas, so don't be shy to add more to your preference.
Leftover Adas thickens in the refrigerator. To reheat, add a splash of water or broth to achieve your desired consistency before warming gently on the stovetop.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East, where vibrant flavors, aromatic spices, and time-honored traditions converge to create dishes that nourish both body and soul. Among the region's diverse array of comforting stews and hearty soups, one stands out for its simplicity, nutritional power, and universally beloved taste: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known locally as Adas. This humble yet incredibly satisfying dish is a staple in Lebanese households, cherished for its warmth, affordability, and the ease with which it transforms basic ingredients into a culinary masterpiece. Whether you're seeking a wholesome weeknight meal, a comforting bowl on a chilly day, or an authentic taste of Lebanese cuisine, this vibrant red lentil soup is an absolute must-try.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas?

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, or Adas (which simply means "lentils" in Arabic), is a quintessential dish in Middle Eastern cooking. It's a vibrant, often creamy, and incredibly flavorful soup made primarily from split red lentils, vegetables, and a generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice. Unlike some other lentil soups that might be thick with meat or heavy cream, Lebanese Adas is typically light, bright, and often vegan or vegetarian, making it accessible to a wide range of dietary preferences.

The magic of this soup lies in its elegant simplicity. It starts with a base of sautéed onions, garlic, and sometimes carrots and potatoes, providing a subtle sweetness and depth. Red lentils, which cook quickly and break down easily, give the soup its signature creamy texture and rich, earthy flavor. Cumin is the dominant spice, lending its warm, aromatic notes that perfectly complement the lentils. What truly elevates this dish, however, is the finishing touch of fresh lemon juice and a drizzle of high-quality olive oil. This combination adds a zesty brightness that cuts through the richness, creating a beautifully balanced and incredibly addictive flavor profile. It's more than just a soup; it's a bowl of warmth, tradition, and an authentic taste of Lebanese home cooking.

## Ingredients You Need

To create your own authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, gather these essential ingredients:

*   	1 cup split red lentils, rinsed thoroughly
*   	1 tablespoon olive oil, plus more for garnish
*   	1 large onion, finely chopped
*   	2-3 cloves garlic, minced
*   	1 carrot, peeled and diced (optional, but adds sweetness)
*   	1 medium potato, peeled and diced (optional, adds body)
*   	6-8 cups vegetable broth or water (adjust for desired consistency)
*   	1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   	1/2 teaspoon salt, or to taste
*   	1/4 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
*   	Juice of 1 large lemon (about 2-3 tablespoons), plus extra wedges for serving
*   	Fresh cilantro or parsley, chopped, for garnish
*   	Pinch of paprika or Aleppo pepper for garnish (optional)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these simple steps to prepare your comforting bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Place the split red lentils in a fine-mesh sieve and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, stirring occasionally.
3.  **Add Garlic and Vegetables:** Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. If using, add the diced carrot and potato, and cook for 3-5 minutes, stirring to coat.
4.  **Introduce Lentils and Spices:** Add the rinsed red lentils, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Stir well to combine all ingredients and toast the spices for about 1 minute, enhancing their aroma.
5.  **Pour in Liquid and Simmer:** Pour in 6 cups of vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Achieve Desired Consistency:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. For a smoother soup, use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup to your desired consistency. If you prefer a chunkier soup, you can skip this step or mash some of the lentils with the back of a spoon. If the soup is too thick, add more hot broth or water until it reaches your preferred consistency.
7.  **Finish with Lemon and Olive Oil:** Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed, adding more salt, pepper, or cumin if desired.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a little extra virgin olive oil, sprinkle with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley, and a pinch of paprika or Aleppo pepper if using. Serve immediately with lemon wedges on the side.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is all about a few key techniques and ingredients:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils:** This is crucial! Rinsing removes starch and any small impurities, ensuring your soup is clear and not cloudy, and preventing a gummy texture.
*   **Sauté Vegetables Properly:** Don't rush the sautéing of the onions and garlic. Caramelizing the onions slightly develops a deeper flavor base for your soup.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice is Non-Negotiable:** The bright, zesty tang of fresh lemon juice is the defining characteristic of this Lebanese Red Lentil Soup. Bottled lemon juice simply won't yield the same vibrant flavor. Add it at the end to preserve its freshness.
*   **Good Quality Olive Oil:** A generous drizzle of good extra virgin olive oil at the end not only adds richness but also enhances the overall flavor profile.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Red lentils absorb a lot of liquid. Have extra hot broth or water on hand to thin the soup to your preferred consistency, especially after blending.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice as needed. Flavors can mellow during cooking, and a final tweak can make all the difference.

## Serving Suggestions

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a wonderfully versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. It makes a fantastic light lunch or a hearty starter for a larger meal.

*   **Fresh Garnishes:** Always serve with a generous sprinkle of fresh cilantro or parsley. A swirl of good quality extra virgin olive oil and a few lemon wedges on the side are essential for that authentic Lebanese touch. A pinch of paprika or Aleppo pepper adds a lovely color and mild heat.
*   **Bread:** It pairs perfectly with warm pita bread for dipping, or a crusty piece of rustic bread.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple, crisp green salad with a lemon-tahini dressing or a classic fattoush salad makes a refreshing accompaniment.
*   **Pickled Vegetables:** Lebanese pickled turnips or cucumbers offer a delightful tangy contrast to the warm, earthy soup.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand.

*   **Add More Vegetables:** Feel free to incorporate other finely diced vegetables like celery, bell peppers, or even a handful of fresh spinach or Swiss chard during the last few minutes of cooking.
*   **Spice It Up:** For a bit of a kick, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or a tiny bit of red pepper flakes along with the cumin. A touch of turmeric can also be added for a subtle earthy flavor and a beautiful golden hue.
*   **Herbs:** While cilantro and parsley are traditional, a sprig of fresh mint added at the end can provide a different, refreshing aroma.
*   **Protein Boost:** For a non-vegetarian option, you can add some shredded cooked chicken or a handful of small meatballs during the last 10 minutes of simmering.
*   **Texture:** If you prefer a completely smooth and velvety soup, blend it until no lumps remain. For a more rustic feel, blend only partially or simply mash some of the lentils with a fork.
*   **Garnish Innovations:** Toasted pine nuts or croutons can add an extra layer of texture.

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is more than just a meal; it's an experience – a warm embrace from the heart of Lebanese culinary tradition. Its simplicity belies a depth of flavor that is both comforting and invigorating. Easy to make, incredibly nutritious, and bursting with bright, earthy notes, this Adas recipe is destined to become a beloved staple in your kitchen. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be enchanted by the timeless allure of this Middle Eastern classic. Sahtein! (To your health!)
